title: Default podman.spec to use crun
body:
On systems with cgroupV2 runc will not currently work
switch the default in spec file to use crun.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
We should make sure this works on Cent 7, though; this needs to work there for the rdocheck tests
The change looks fine to me, I'm just a little concerned about distros without v2.  Can we get a head nod from @lsm5?
crun works fine on non V2 systems, I did move the crun requirements and oci-runtime requirement inside of the Fedora block.

LGTM RE: crun.

On Fedora, I noticed the crun package `Provides: oci-runtime = 2`. So I think we should get rid of oci-runtime from podman spec as `Requires: oci-runtime` and `Recommends: crun` together doesn't seem right.

Not sure what that means, oci-runtime=2 versus oci-runtime=1?  Bottom line podman requires an OCI Runtime to be installed.  I want that to be allowed to only install runc, crun, or kata-runtime.  I would prefer to have all three of these be provides oci-runtime, and then the user of podman could remove the others as he pleases.  Without requires oci-runtime, then the user could remove all runtimes just by eliminating reccomends, and stuf will break.
